_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL - PTERODACTYL.IO
name: Next.js
author: https://nyxel.my.id
description: Host a Next.js application with auto-update, private repo support, both dev/production modes, optional Cloudflare Tunnel, and configurable package manager.
startup: bash -c "mkdir -p /home/container/.pterodactyl && curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Synxx12/nextjs-egg/main/entrypoint.sh -o /home/container/.pterodactyl/entrypoint.sh || { echo '[EGG] Failed to download entrypoint!'; exit 1; } && chmod +x /home/container/.pterodactyl/entrypoint.sh && bash /home/container/.pterodactyl/entrypoint.sh"

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Next.js Installation Script — nyxel

set -e

apt-get update -y
apt-get install -y git curl jq file unzip make gcc g++ python3 python3-dev libtool

## ── Write runtime entrypoint script ────────────────────────────────────────
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.pterodactyl

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Synxx12/nextjs-egg/main/entrypoint.sh -o /mnt/server/.pterodactyl/entrypoint.sh
chmod +x /mnt/server/.pterodactyl/entrypoint.sh
echo "[INSTALL] entrypoint.sh downloaded to .pterodactyl/"

## ── Clone repository (only if GIT_URL is set) ───────────────────────────────
cd /mnt/server

if [ -n "${GIT_URL}" ]; then
  if [ -n "${USERNAME}" ] && [ -n "${ACCESS_TOKEN}" ]; then
    GIT_URL="https://${USERNAME}:${ACCESS_TOKEN}@$(echo ${GIT_URL} | sed 's|https://||')"
  fi

  if [ -d /mnt/server/.git ]; then
    echo "[INSTALL] Git repo already present — pulling latest..."
    git reset --hard
    git pull
  else
    echo "[INSTALL] Wiping directory and cloning fresh..."
    find /mnt/server -mindepth 1 -not -path '/mnt/server/.pterodactyl*' -delete 2>/dev/null || true
    if [ -z "${GIT_BRANCH}" ]; then
      git clone "${GIT_URL}" /tmp/repo_clone
    else
      git clone --single-branch --branch "${GIT_BRANCH}" "${GIT_URL}" /tmp/repo_clone
    fi
    cp -a /tmp/repo_clone/. /mnt/server/
    rm -rf /tmp/repo_clone
  fi

  if [ -f /mnt/server/package.json ]; then
    echo "[INSTALL] Installing Node.js dependencies..."
    cd /mnt/server
    if [ -f /mnt/server/package-lock.json ]; then
      /usr/local/bin/npm ci
    else
      /usr/local/bin/npm install
    fi
  fi
else
  echo "[INSTALL] GIT_URL is empty — skipping clone. Upload your project files via File Manager."
fi

echo "[INSTALL] Done."
exit 0

variables.name: Git Repository URL
variables.description: HTTPS URL to your Git repository (e.g. https://github.com/user/repo). Leave empty to skip cloning — you can upload project files manually via the File Manager.

variables.name: Git Branch
variables.description: Branch to clone and run (e.g. main, master, production). Leave empty to use the repository default branch.

variables.name: Auto Update on Restart
variables.description: Set to 1 to automatically pull the latest code from your repository every time the server starts. Set to 0 to keep the current version (faster startup). Recommended: 1 for active development, 0 for stable production.

variables.name: Git Username
variables.description: Your GitHub or GitLab username. Only needed for private repositories. Leave empty if your repo is public.

variables.name: Git Access Token
variables.description: Personal access token for private repositories (github.com/settings/tokens — requires 'repo' scope). Leave empty for public repos. Keep this secret — do not share it with anyone.

variables.name: Run Mode
variables.description: Set to 'production' to build and serve a fully optimized app (next build + next start) — use this for live/public sites. Set to 'development' for hot-reload dev mode (next dev) — use this while coding. Note: production mode takes longer to start because it builds first.

variables.name: Package Manager
variables.description: Which package manager to use for installing dependencies. 'auto' will detect automatically based on your lockfile (pnpm-lock.yaml → pnpm, yarn.lock → yarn, otherwise npm). Options: auto, npm, pnpm, yarn.

variables.name: Skip Build
variables.description: Set to 1 to skip 'next build' on startup. Only use this if you have already uploaded a pre-built .next/ folder via File Manager (e.g. built locally or via CI). If no .next/ is found, the server will build anyway. Default: 0 (always build on start).

variables.name: Custom Build Command
variables.description: Override the default build command. Leave empty to use the standard 'next build'. Only change this if you have a monorepo or special setup (e.g. 'turbo build --filter=web', 'next build --experimental-turbo'). Most users should leave this empty.

variables.name: Public Domain
variables.description: Your public-facing domain (e.g. https://www.example.com). Required when using Cloudflare Tunnel or any reverse proxy. The egg will automatically set NEXTAUTH_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL in your .env file on startup, preventing redirect issues (e.g. being redirected to localhost after login/logout). Leave empty if not using a custom domain.

variables.name: Cloudflare Tunnel Token
variables.description: Optional. Your Cloudflare Tunnel token from the Zero Trust dashboard (Networks → Tunnels → Configure → Run token). When set, the server will be accessible via your Cloudflare domain without opening any ports. Leave empty to disable.
